response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned generalist with 15+ years across web, mobile, and systems work, but his career is centered on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and full-stack product development. His only professional C++ experience is a roughly seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015, and he shows no exposure to industrial automation, MQTT/IoT protocols, message-broker architectures at the required depth, or IEC 62443 / Secure Development Lifecycle practices. His low-latency DLang work demonstrates transferable concurrency skills, but the gap on the role's core C++ and industrial-security requirements is too large for a senior, mentoring-oriented C++ position.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, embedded, and desktop systems
Hands-on low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching) handling millions of requests per second
Strong engineering culture: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and experience teaching/mentoring through workshops
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience (~7 months, ending in 2015); recent decade is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang
No industrial automation, IoT protocol (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus), or IEC 62443 / SDL security background
Recent roles are web/full-stack product engineering, not the networked multi-threaded C++ architecture this role demands
